attributes.stem:
A 57-year-old woman presents to the GP with a three month history of irregular vaginal bleeding. She went through the menopause at the age of 49 and has not any bleeding until the last three months. In the past her menses were irregular and heavy. She denies fatigue, weight loss or abdominal bloating. She never married or had any children.

Her medical history is significant for breast cancer diagnosed three years ago. She is currently on tamoxifen with a plan to continue taking it for another 7 years. She had a low risk cervical screening test (CST) one year ago. The physical examination, including pelvic examination, is normal however the cervix is noted to be stenosed and flush with the vaginal walls. The patient states that the speculum examination is too uncomfortable and asks you to stop.

An ultrasound is performed. A thickened endometrium of 13mm with cystic spaces is reported. The right and left ovaries are small and no abnormal masses or free fluid are seen.
attributes.stemQuestion: Which one of the following is the most appropriate next step in management?
attributes.explanation:
The presence of post menopausal bleeding (PMB) is an abnormal symptom and ongoing bleeding requires investigation with the primary differential diagnosis being endometrial cancer.

Tamoxifen is a selective oestrogen receptor modulator (SERM) which has anti-oestrogenic actions on the breast tissue and weak agonistic properties on the endometrium resulting in a 2-7 fold increase in endometrial hyperplasia, atypia and endometrial cancer. Hysteroscopy, dilatation and curettage (D&C) would be the most appropriate next step in management to assess endometrial cavity and sample a large amount of endometrial tissue for histopathology. This is for 2 reasons, the patient is unlikely to tolerate a pipelle examination if she consented for repeat speculum exam and secondly, direct examination and larger endometrial sampling will give more histopathology and clinical information.

A repeat CST would be the incorrect test as there would only be liquid based cytological (LBC) assessment if there was human papilloma virus (HPV) detected. Abnormal endometrial cells are occasionally detected incidentally on CST/LBC and a hysteroscopy D&C is then indicated.

A CT pelvis can be requested as a staging test for endometrial cancer, once histopathological diagnosis has been made and the patient is being investigated extrauterine metastases. CT pelvis gives poor definition of ovarian and endometrial tissues and is not used as a first line investigation for PMB.

MRI pelvis is becoming increasingly common for the investigation of complex ovarian masses (to aid differentiation between endometriomas and ovarian malignancies) as well as the staging of cervical and endometrial cancers. It is not the firstline investigation for PMB as whilst it gives similar definition and information about the endometrium as ultrasound, it is not currently funded by Medicare and patients are required to pay an out of pocket expense.

A pipelle endometrial biopsy may be considered in patients in gynaecology clinic (not a GP setting) who will tolerate the procedure and have a cervix open enough to allow the pipelle to pass. This patient has reported significant discomfort with the speculum examination and cervical stenosis making it unlikely that a pipelle would pass through the cervical Os. D&C done under general anaesthesia allows for gentle dilation of the cervix and directed sampling with hysteroscopy.
learningPoints: The possibility of an underlying malignancy must always be considered in cases of post menopausal bleeding.
references: Tamoxifen and uterine cancer. Committee Opinion No. 601. American College of Obstetricians and Gynecologists. Obstet Gynecol 2014;123:1394–7
Emons G, Mustea A, Tempfer C. Tamoxifen and Endometrial Cancer: A Janus-Headed Drug. Cancers (Basel). 2020 Sep 7;12(9):2535. doi: 10.3390/cancers12092535. PMID: 32906618; PMCID: PMC7564212.

Neven P, Froyman W, Timmerman S, Timmerman D. Uterine ultrasound and endometrial biopsy in tamoxifen users. Breast Cancer Res Treat. 2020 Apr;180(3):833-834. doi: 10.1007/s10549-020-05595-5. Epub 2020 Mar 16. PMID: 32180073
Sadro CT. Imaging the Endometrium: A Pictorial Essay. Can Assoc Radiol J. 2016 Aug;67(3):254-62. doi: 10.1016/j.carj.2015.09.012. Epub 2016 Apr 15. PMID: 27090929.
